description: Schema-driven corpus for graphql-security. No deterministic scanner captures depth/complexity/introspection/batching semantics, so the runner smoke-validates shape; fixtures double as ground truth for the LLM eval harness. detect = a GraphQL server config that violates a NEVER rule (missing cost limit, prod introspection, unbounded batching); ignore = the hardened equivalent following ALWAYS.

fixtures.reason: no maximum query depth or complexity/cost limit; a 5-level nested query against a many-to-many edge returns billions of nodes and exhausts the database (CWE-400 Uncontrolled Resource Consumption)
enforces a max query depth (8) and a complexity/cost ceiling (1000) at the server, bounding resource consumption (mitigates CWE-400)
introspection and the GraphiQL playground are left enabled in production, handing attackers the full schema for reconnaissance (CWE-200 Information Exposure)
introspection and the landing page playground are disabled when NODE_ENV is production; legitimate clients use schema codegen instead (follows ALWAYS: disable introspection in production)

batched HTTP requests are allowed with no cap on operations-per-batch and no alias limit, giving an attacker an N-fold request-amplification / brute-force primitive (CWE-400, CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits)
caps aliases per request (15) and operations per batch (5), removing the amplification primitive while still permitting legitimate batching (follows ALWAYS: limit aliases and batch operations)
